title: MigrationOutcome::Failed wraps anyhow::Error which is not Clone limiting reporting
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/migrations/mod.rs:38-45
## Violation
MigrationOutcome::Failed holds an anyhow::Error:

```rust
pub enum MigrationOutcome {
    Skipped,
    Applied(String),
    Failed(anyhow::Error),
}
```

anyhow::Error does not implement Clone. Callers of run_all that want to aggregate / persist / display the result set cannot do so without consuming the Vec<MigrationReport>. The Vec is returned by value so this works once, but a CLI subcommand like `openclaudia migrate --dry-run --json` (mentioned in the description's doc comment) would need to print the errors AND optionally save them — currently impossible without consuming twice.

Additionally, MigrationOutcome implements no Display / Debug derivations (anyhow's Display works manually, but the enum itself can't derive Debug because anyhow::Error doesn't impl Debug uniformly).

## Mandated refactor
Either:
1. Store String (the error message) in Failed:
```rust
Failed(String),  // captured via format!("{:#}", err) at the call site
```
2. Or define a typed migration error enum with thiserror so each variant has known structure.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
